commit.message:
Fix transpiler failure with small constants in peek/poke (#228)

* Initial plan

* Fix peek/poke with small constants (ldc.i4.s) by adjusting RemoveLastInstructions count

When peek() or poke() is called with a small address constant (≤255), the C# compiler
emits ldc.i4.s (1-byte form) instead of ldc.i4 (4-byte form). WriteLdc(byte) emits only
1 instruction (LDA) vs WriteLdc(ushort) which emits 2 (LDX + LDA). The peek/poke handlers
had hardcoded RemoveLastInstructions counts assuming the ushort path, causing them to remove
too many instructions and corrupt the code stream.

Fix: make the removal count dynamic based on whether addr > byte.MaxValue.

Co-authored-by: jonathanpeppers <840039+jonathanpeppers@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve test comments for clarity on ldc.i4.s threshold

Co-authored-by: jonathanpeppers <840039+jonathanpeppers@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reword test comments to describe byte-sized constant load path

Address review feedback: the comments incorrectly attributed the behavior
to ldc.i4.s (<= 255 threshold). The actual transpiler condition is whether
the address fits in a byte (WriteLdc(byte) emits 1 instruction vs
WriteLdc(ushort) emitting 2), checked via addr > byte.MaxValue.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Detect closure structs from local functions and throw helpful TranspileException (#231)

* Initial plan

* Detect closure structs from local functions and throw helpful TranspileException

When local functions capture outer byte[] variables, the C# compiler generates
closure structs (display classes). The transpiler now detects these early in
DetectStructLayouts() and throws a TranspileException with clear guidance:
- Explains what closures are and why they happen
- Suggests passing captured variables as parameters instead
- Suggests inlining the local function code

Also improves the fallback error in ResolveStructType() and the Newobj message
to mention closures as a possible cause.

commit.message:
Fix explosion position: cross-array copy in stelem.i1 (#230)

* Initial plan

* Fix cross-array copy pattern in HandleStelemI1 for correct explosion positioning

The transpiler's HandleStelemI1 didn't handle the pattern arr1[i] = arr2[j]
(cross-array copy with different indices). For shoot2's explosion code
(exp_x[xi] = enemy_x[ei]), no LDA was emitted so the stored value was
whatever was left in A from a previous operation.

Added source index tracking (sourceIndex1Idx) in the ldelem scan and a new
pattern handler that emits: LDX srcIdx; LDA srcArr,X; (then existing store
code handles LDX dstIdx; STA dstArr,X).

Co-authored-by: jonathanpeppers <840039+jonathanpeppers@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix potential KeyNotFoundException in cross-array sourceIndex1Idx lookup

Only set sourceIndex1Idx when Locals.TryGetValue succeeds and the local
is a scalar (ArraySize == 0). Previously, the condition also accepted
untracked locals (!TryGetValue), which would throw KeyNotFoundException
when the cross-array handler later accessed Locals[sourceIndex1Idx].

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add Azure Bicep modules for Secure Research Environment

Implements a complete IaC solution for a regulated-data Secure Research
Environment based on the Azure reference architecture:
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/ai-ml/architecture/secure-compute-for-research

Architecture:
- Two resource groups: {env}-network-rg and {env}-compute-rg
- VNet with 4 subnets (ResearchersSubnet, ComputeSubnet,
  PrivateEndpointSubnet, DataIntegrationSubnet) and per-subnet NSGs
- 4 private DNS zones linked to the VNet for blob, vault, ADF, AzureML
- Public ingestion storage -> Azure Data Factory (managed VNet, private
  endpoint) -> immutable private secure storage
- Azure Virtual Desktop session hosts (AAD join, hardened RDP policy)
  in ResearchersSubnet -> Ubuntu 22.04 DSVM(s) in ComputeSubnet
- Logic App HTTP-webhook egress approval workflow
- Key Vault (RBAC, purge protection, private endpoint)
- Log Analytics (PerGB2018) with diagnostic settings on all resources
- Azure Monitor agent on Data Science VMs
- RBAC role assignments for ADF managed identity

Files:
  bicep/main.bicep                              subscription-scoped orchestration
  bicep/main.bicepparam                         example parameters
  bicep/modules/monitoring/logAnalytics.bicep   Log Analytics workspace
  bicep/modules/network/vnet.bicep              VNet, NSGs, DNS zones
  bicep/modules/keyvault/keyvault.bicep         Key Vault + private endpoint
  bicep/modules/storage/storageIngestion.bicep  public ingestion storage
  bicep/modules/storage/storageSecure.bicep     private secure storage
  bicep/modules/datafactory/datafactory.bicep   ADF + managed VNet + role assignments
  bicep/modules/avd/avd.bicep                   AVD host pool, app group, workspace + VMs
  bicep/modules/compute/datasciencevm.bicep     Ubuntu DSVM(s) with AMA + AAD login
  bicep/modules/logicapp/egressApproval.bicep   egress approval Logic App

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix repeating EXC_BAD_ACCESS crash in PKMSync menu bar app (#136)

* Fix repeating EXC_BAD_ACCESS crash in PKMSync menu bar app

Remove .symbolEffect(.rotate) from MenuBarExtra label and popover
status header — continuous CoreAnimation on the label triggers
_NSWindowTransformAnimation lifecycle issues in AppKit's internal
window management, causing use-after-free during autorelease pool
drain.

Additional fixes:
- Use distinct SF Symbol for syncing state instead of animation
- Add orderOut(nil) before close() on diff window to suppress
  close animation and prevent stale animation references
- Remove double MenuBarViewModel initialization in PKMSyncApp
- Remove redundant startScheduler() call (already in MenuBarView.task)

* docs: fix Lambda function count and add macOS CI workflows

- README.md: 42 → 43 deployed Lambda functions
- CLAUDE.md: add macos-build.yml and macos-test.yml to workflows list

* Restore startScheduler() call at app init

The MenuBarView .task only fires when the popover is shown, so the
scheduler must be started at init for background syncing to work
without requiring the user to open the popover first.

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add coding style and coding guardrails guides

commit.message:
Traverse any-list children in all Other* AST nodes

Previously, all Other* handlers (OtherStmt, OtherExpr, OtherType,
OtherPat, OtherParam, OtherArg, OtherAttribute, OtherCatch,
OtherCond, OtherModule, OtherTypeKind, OtherTypeArg, OtherDef,
OtherCase, OtherDirective, OtherStmtWithStmt) discarded their
`any list` children with a wildcard pattern, emitting only a
stub string.

This meant OCaml function bodies (wrapped as OtherStmt/ExprStmt2)
and many other language-specific constructs produced no child
TRAP entries — expressions, statements, patterns, types, etc.
were all lost.

Add emit_any_child/emit_any_children to recursively traverse
the any type (expressions, statements, types, patterns, names,
definitions, directives, parameters, arguments, attributes,
fields, catch exceptions, module defs, entities) and emit them
as field_1 children of their parent Other* node.

Result: trap_visitor.ml extraction goes from 1,928 to 59,605
TRAP lines — a 30x increase matching expected CodeQL output size.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Linux CI: don't pin conf-libpcre/conf-tree-sitter

Pinning overrides the system packages (libpcre3-dev, libtree-sitter-dev)
with our static-only builds which lack -fPIC and fail when ocamlmklib
tries to create shared stubs. Only pin and install from source on macOS

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix empty benchmark results: NIST CTC manifest YAML block scalar breaks custom parser (#42)

* Initial plan

* Fix empty benchmark results: convert NIST CTC manifest summary from YAML block scalar to single-line string

The custom YAML subset parser in fixture_manifest.cc does not support
YAML block scalars (> or |). The nist-ctc/manifest.yaml used:

  summary: >
    NIST MBE PMI Conformance Test Case ...

The parser reads the second indented line and throws "Unexpected
indentation", which bench_navigator catches as a hard manifest parse
error. Because HasErrors() returns true, bench_navigator exits with
EXIT_FAILURE before printing the "=== Fixture Ray Benchmark Results ==="
header. The generate_bench_report.py parser finds no header and renders
an empty page.

Fix: convert the summary to a single-line quoted string, consistent with
all other family manifests (boolean-compound, direct-primitives,
profile-faceted, tessellated, twisted-swept, wrapper-decorator).

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

never tagged any ships (gated on knownYards.length > 0) — chicken-and-egg.

commit.message:
Fix macOS CI: set PKG_CONFIG_PATH for opam prefix

On macOS, pcre and tree-sitter are built from source by our conf-*
opam packages. The pkg-config .pc files are installed into the opam
prefix, but pkg-config doesn't search there by default. Add a step
to export PKG_CONFIG_PATH so subsequent opam installs (pcre OCaml
bindings) and dune builds can find the libraries.

Also install conf-libpcre and conf-tree-sitter explicitly before
other deps to ensure headers are available when pcre OCaml bindings
compile.

commit.message:
Add three.js point-cloud visualization of geantino ray tests to CI artifacts (#40)

* Initial plan

* Add three.js point-cloud visualization of geantino ray tests

- Extend FixtureRayComparisonOptions with point_cloud_dir: when set,
  CompareFixtureRays writes a per-fixture JSON file containing
  pre_step_origin and native/imported post-step surface hit points
- Add WritePointCloudJson / JsonString / WriteJsonVectorArray helpers
  in fixture_ray_compare.cc (anonymous namespace, <fstream> added)
- bench_navigator accepts an optional third positional arg for the
  point-cloud output directory
- New .github/scripts/generate_point_cloud_report.py generates a
  self-contained HTML viewer backed by three.js (v0.169, CDN) with
  OrbitControls, fixture dropdown, native/imported toggle, and stats
- ci.yml: run bench_navigator with point-cloud export, generate bench
  and point-cloud HTML reports, upload as ci-reports artifact

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

* Refactor point-cloud report: separate CSS/JS files + Jinja2 template

Extract embedded CSS and JS from generate_point_cloud_report.py into
proper tracked files:
- point_cloud_viewer.css  – styles with CSS SPDX header
- point_cloud_viewer.js   – three.js viewer; reads fixture data from
  a <script type="application/json" id="fixture-data"> DOM element,
  removing the /*FIXTURE_DATA*/ string replacement entirely
- point_cloud_viewer.html.jinja2 – Jinja2 HTML template that inlines
  the CSS/JS and embeds the per-run fixture JSON

generate_point_cloud_report.py now uses Jinja2 (Environment +
FileSystemLoader) to render the template.  ci.yml installs jinja2
before the report-generation step.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

* Apply review feedback on point-cloud export and viewer

- fixture_ray_compare.cc: JsonString() escapes control chars (\\uXXXX,
  uppercase hex per RFC 8259); include <cstdio> for snprintf
- fixture_ray_compare.cc: fixture_id in JSON is now family-qualified
  (family/id) so viewer dropdown grouping works correctly
- fixture_ray_compare.cc: filename sanitization replaces all
  non-(alnum/hyphen/period) chars with '_'; drops undocumented
  "only alphanumerics" comment
- fixture_ray_compare.cc: WritePointCloudJson exports separate
  native_pre_step_origin and imported_pre_step_origin fields to
  eliminate metadata inconsistency
- point_cloud_viewer.js: clearClouds() now disposes both geometry and
  material to prevent GPU resource leaks across fixture loads
- point_cloud_viewer.js: camera fitting prefers currently-visible cloud
  (respects showNative/showImported flags)
- point_cloud_viewer.js: stats panel shows both native and imported
  origins
- point_cloud_viewer.html.jinja2: SPDX header changed from Jinja2
  {# #} comments to HTML <!-- --> form for enarx/spdx CI compliance
- generate_point_cloud_report.py: fixture_json escapes '</' -> '<\/'
  before template injection to prevent </script> injection; docstring
  drops "self-contained" claim and documents CDN dependency; key
  validation updated for new origins schema

Makes panels draggable by a handle element. Matches the original
Ajax Control Toolkit DragPanelExtender behavior.

- Handles edge cases (text selection, left-click only)

Allows users to resize elements by dragging a handle. Matches the original
Ajax Control Toolkit ResizableControlExtender behavior.
